title: Limit Concurrent Async Tasks with Semaphore
description: Use asyncio.Semaphore to limit the number of concurrent HTTP requests, preventing resource exhaustion and server overload

before.code:
# UNOPTIMIZED: Unbounded concurrency
async def scrape(self, urls: CreateRecipeByUrlBulk) -> None:
    async def _do(url: str) -> Recipe | None:
        #  PROBLEM: No limit on concurrent requests!
        # If urls.imports has 100 items, all 100 HTTP requests
        # fire simultaneously, overwhelming the server and network
        try:
            recipe, _ = await create_from_html(url, self.translator)
            return recipe
        except Exception as e:
            self._add_error_entry(f"failed to scrape url {url}", str(e))
            return None

    tasks = [_do(b.url) for b in urls.imports]
    #  All tasks start at once - unbounded parallelism
    results = await gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=False)
    # ... process results

after.code:
# OPTIMIZED: Bounded concurrency with Semaphore
async def scrape(self, urls: CreateRecipeByUrlBulk) -> None:
    #  FIX: Limit to 3 concurrent requests
    sem = asyncio.Semaphore(3)

    async def _do(url: str) -> Recipe | None:
        #  Acquire semaphore before making request
        async with sem:
            try:
                recipe, _ = await create_from_html(url, self.translator)
                return recipe
            except Exception as e:
                self._add_error_entry(f"failed to scrape url {url}", str(e))
                return None

    tasks = [_do(b.url) for b in urls.imports]
    # Now only 3 requests run at a time
    results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
    # ... process results

energy_impact: Reduces peak resource usage by limiting concurrent connections. Prevents network congestion and server overload. More predictable memory usage.
key_insight: Use asyncio.Semaphore(N) to limit concurrent async operations. Wrap the async work inside 'async with sem:' block.
